meta.title: Video by jinabaobina
meta.description:
Fine dining Korean fried chicken in NYC

This spot was such a fun experience. The service, the vibe, the attention to detail… everything felt elevated. Even the chicken itself felt higher quality. Better batter, better oil, just more refined overall.

I will say, I’ve had better Korean fried chicken flavor wise. This place is less about the best chicken you’ll ever have, and more about the full experience of it all.
I’d definitely come back with friends.

📍 Coqodaq @coqodaq 

Everything I ordered: 

Black Gold Nugget $30 per nugget. It was good. You can taste the quality and the truffle, but I personally can’t justify $30 for one nugget again

Caesar Salad $19�Solid, exactly what you’d want. Well dressed and balanced

The Bucket List $46 per person. You can split all 3 flavors or customize it. We tried original, soy garlic, and gochujang. Comes with banchan, sauces, perilla seed noodles, and frozen yogurt which made it feel like a full experience

The Egg $23: I don’t drink, but this was one of my favorite parts. The lychee “egg” was so unique, almost like a sorbet texture. I’d honestly come back just for this and would 100% ask if they can make it as a mocktail

#koreanfriedchicken #newyorkfood #nyceats #koreanfood #nycfood

transcript.transcript: This is the most luxurious Korean fried chicken spot in New York. Actually, possibly in the world. For our trip, Keenan made a list of places he really wanted us to try. This was one I went into completely blind and I was shocked. Just look at this place. We started with the black gold truffle nuggets. Yes. $30 per nugget. I tried it so you don't have to. And it's good but absolutely not worth it. If you're getting a drink, the egg is actually really good. I don't drink but I honestly just ask for the leechie egg which is the ice cube shaped like a egg and made of leechie puree. Kind of like a leechie sorbet. The Caesar salad was solid. Exactly what you want. Coded perfectly. But these really unique croutons that added a nice crunch and lots of parmesan. For the main, we got the bucket list. $46 per person. We wanted to try all three flavors. Original soy garlic and guchijang. It also comes with pung chan, house made sauces, per leeched noodles and frozen yogurt. Out of all three, I like the original the most. You really get to appreciate how crispy it is. And you can switch up the flavors with the sauces. I do wish we got the duck bookie instead of the per leech. The noodles didn't really have much flavor. I would have preferred a mooning and vinegary punch to balance with the rest of the meal. Overall, the service was amazing. The space, beautiful and the quality is there. Definitely a cool spot to hit up on the weekend for dinner with friends. Would you try kukutak?
